title: Senecio masafuerae
taxonomy: Senecio masafuerae is a plant species in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Senecio, which comprises over 2,000 species of plants commonly known as ragworts or groundsels. Senecio masafuerae is a type of flowering plant native to the Juan Fernández Islands in Chile. The plant's classification is further divided into the family Asteraceae, tribe Senecioneae, and genus Senecio. More specifically, its classification is: Senecio masafuerae, Asteraceae, Senecioneae, Senecio.

intro: Senecio masafuerae, also known as Masafuera ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Juan Fernández Islands, where it has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Senecio masafuerae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Senecio masafuerae is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments.
